sentences.chinese: 虜騎數千追之，韓延年戰死。
sentences.literal: Several thousand Hu riders pursued them; Han Yannian died in battle.
sentences.idiomatic: Thousands of enemy horse overtook them; Han Yannian fell fighting.

sentences.chinese: 陵曰：「無面目報陛下！」
sentences.literal: Ling said, "I have no face to report to His Majesty!"
sentences.idiomatic: Li Ling cried, "I cannot face the emperor again!"

sentences.chinese: 遂降。
sentences.literal: Thereupon he surrendered.
sentences.idiomatic: He yielded to the Xiongnu.

sentences.chinese: 軍人分散，脫至塞者四百餘人。
sentences.literal: The soldiers scattered apart; those who escaped and reached the barrier were more than four hundred.
sentences.idiomatic: The command broke and ran; a little over four hundred men won through to the frontier.

sentences.chinese: 陵敗處去塞百餘里，邊塞以聞。
sentences.literal: The place where Ling was defeated was more than a hundred li from the barrier; the border garrisons reported it.
sentences.idiomatic: The field where Li Ling went down lay only a hundred-odd li from the passes, and couriers soon brought word to court.

sentences.chinese: 上欲陵死戰，召陵母及婦，使相者視之，無死喪色。
sentences.literal: The emperor wished Ling to fight to the death; he summoned Ling's mother and wife and had physiognomists examine them—they showed no color of death or mourning.
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor had wanted Li Ling to fight to the finish; he even called in Li Ling's mother and wife for face-readers to inspect, and neither bore the marks of imminent bereavement.

sentences.chinese: 後聞陵降，上怒甚，責問陳步樂，步樂自殺。
sentences.literal: Later hearing Ling had surrendered, the emperor was greatly angry, harshly questioned Chen Bule, and Bule killed himself.
sentences.idiomatic: When the news of surrender came, the emperor's fury fell on Chen Bule, who had praised Li Ling's troops; under interrogation he took his own life.

sentences.chinese: 群臣皆罪陵，上以問太史令司馬遷，遷盛言：「陵事親孝，與士信，常奮不顧身以殉國家之急。
sentences.literal: The various ministers all blamed Ling; the emperor thereupon asked Grand Astrologer Sima Qian, and Qian spoke at length, saying, "Ling served his parents with filial piety, with officers is trustworthy, and constantly exerted himself without regard for his person to meet the state's urgent need.
sentences.idiomatic: The whole court denounced Li Ling, so the emperor turned to Sima Qian, who spoke up at length: Li Ling had been a dutiful son, a commander his men trusted, a man who had repeatedly risked his life for the realm.

sentences.chinese: 其素所畜積也，有國士之風。
sentences.literal: What he has long stored up within him has the manner of a knight of the state.
sentences.idiomatic: That was the man he had always been—a true knight of the empire.

sentences.chinese: 今舉事一不幸，全軀保妻子之臣隨而媒糵其短，誠可痛也！
sentences.literal: Now one mischance in the undertaking, and ministers who preserve their persons and protect wives and children follow brewing his faults—truly it is lamentable!
sentences.idiomatic: One reverse, and every courtier who cares only for his own skin and his family is ready to brew slander against him—it is shameful.

sentences.chinese: 且陵提步卒不滿五千，深輮戎馬之地，抑數萬之師，虜救死扶傷不暇，悉舉引弓之民共攻圍之。
sentences.literal: Moreover Ling led fewer than five thousand foot soldiers deep into country dominated by enemy horse, checked a host of tens of thousands; the captives had no leisure to tend their wounded and dead, and mustered every bowman of the realm to besiege him.
sentences.idiomatic: Consider: he took fewer than five thousand foot deep into cavalry country, held off tens of thousands, and kept the enemy so busy tending their wounded that the Chanyu had to call up every archer on the steppe to ring him in.

sentences.chinese: 轉鬪千里，矢盡道窮，士張空拳，冒白刃，北首爭死敵，得人之死力，雖古名將不過也。
sentences.literal: Turning and fighting a thousand li, arrows exhausted and the road exhausted, soldiers stretched empty fists, braved white blades, north-facing contended to die against the foe—obtaining men's death-effort—even ancient famous generals did not surpass this.
sentences.idiomatic: They fought a retreat of a thousand li until shafts and road alike gave out; his men faced naked steel with bare hands, turning north to die on the enemy—such devotion would have honored any general in history.

sentences.chinese: 身雖䧟敗，然其所摧敗亦足暴於天下。
sentences.literal: Though his person met defeat and ruin, yet what he crushed and defeated is also sufficient to display to the world.
sentences.idiomatic: Though Li Ling himself was broken, the damage he dealt the Xiongnu was enough to resound across the realm.

sentences.chinese: 彼之不死，宜欲得當以報漢也。」
sentences.literal: His not choosing death now likely means he waits for the right moment to repay the Han."
sentences.idiomatic: His staying alive probably means he hopes for another chance to serve the empire."

sentences.chinese: 初，上遣貳師大軍出，財令陵為助兵，及陵與單于相值，而貳師功少。
sentences.literal: In the beginning, when the emperor dispatched the Ershi great army out, he only ordered Ling as auxiliary troops; when Ling confronted the Chanyu straight on, yet Ershi's merit was small.
sentences.idiomatic: At first the emperor had meant Li Ling only to support the main Ershi expedition; when Li Ling met the Chanyu in person, the Ershi column had little to show for itself.

sentences.chinese: 上以遷誣罔，欲沮貳師，為陵遊說，下遷腐刑。
sentences.literal: The emperor thought Qian deceived and falsely accused, wishing to thwart Ershi, pleading for Ling, and handed Qian down to mutilating punishment.
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor read Sima Qian's defense as slander meant to undermine the Ershi general and cover for Li Ling; Sima Qian was sentenced to castration.

sentences.chinese: 久之，上悔陵無救，曰：「陵當發出塞，乃詔強弩都尉令迎軍。
sentences.literal: After a long time the emperor regretted that Ling had no rescue, saying, "When Ling ought to have set out beyond the barrier, then I edicted the crossbow commandant to order meeting the army.
sentences.idiomatic: In time the emperor regretted leaving Li Ling unsupported: "He was already beyond the passes when I ordered the commandant of strong crossbowmen to move up and cover his retreat.

sentences.chinese: 坐預詔之，得令老將生奸詐。」
sentences.literal: Because I pre-edicted him, it made the old general produce deceit."
sentences.idiomatic: By issuing that order too early I only gave an old fox room for intrigue."

sentences.chinese: 乃遣使勞賜陵餘軍得脫者。
sentences.literal: Thereupon he dispatched envoys to console and bestow gifts on those of Ling's remaining army who had escaped.
sentences.idiomatic: He then sent envoys with rewards to the survivors of Li Ling's command who had reached Han soil.

sentences.chinese: 陵在匈奴歲餘，上遣因杅將軍公孫敖將兵深入匈奴迎陵。
sentences.literal: Ling had been among the Xiongnu more than a year when the emperor dispatched General Yangan Gongsun Ao to lead troops deep into the Xiongnu to welcome Ling.
sentences.idiomatic: After Li Ling had spent a year with the Xiongnu, the emperor sent Gongsun Ao, general of Yangan, on a deep raid to bring him home.

sentences.chinese: 敖軍無功還，曰：「捕得生口，言李陵教單于為兵以備漢軍，故臣無所得。」
sentences.literal: Ao's army returned without merit, saying, "We captured a prisoner who said Li Ling teaches the Chanyu to drill troops against the Han army; therefore your servant obtained nothing."
sentences.idiomatic: Gongsun Ao came back empty-handed and reported that prisoners claimed Li Ling was training Xiongnu troops for war on Han—which was why he had found no trace of him.

sentences.chinese: 上聞，於是族陵家，母弟妻子皆伏誅。
sentences.literal: The emperor hearing this, thereupon exterminated Ling's clan; mother, younger brothers, wife, and children all submitted to execution.
sentences.idiomatic: Believing it, the emperor wiped out Li Ling's entire family—mother, brothers, wife, and children all executed.

sentences.chinese: 隴西士大夫以李氏為愧。
sentences.literal: The gentlemen and great families of Longxi took the Li surname as a shame.
sentences.idiomatic: The gentry of Longxi were ashamed to bear the name Li.

sentences.chinese: 其後，漢遣使使匈奴，陵謂使者曰：「吾為漢將步卒五千人橫行匈奴，以亡救而敗，何負於漢而誅吾家？」
sentences.literal: Afterward the Han dispatched an envoy to the Xiongnu; Ling said to the envoy, "I for the Han led foot soldiers five thousand who ranged across the Xiongnu; because there was no rescue I was defeated—what fault toward the Han that my family was executed?"
sentences.idiomatic: Later, when a Han envoy reached the steppe, Li Ling confronted him: "I led five thousand foot for the empire deep in Xiongnu territory and lost only because no relief came. What crime did my family commit?"

sentences.chinese: 使者曰：「漢聞李少卿教匈奴為兵。」
sentences.literal: The envoy said, "The Han heard that Li Shaoqing teaches the Xiongnu to drill troops."
sentences.idiomatic: The envoy answered, "Court rumor said Li Shaoqing was drilling Xiongnu soldiers."

sentences.chinese: 陵曰：「乃李緒，非我也。」
sentences.literal: Ling said, "That was Li Xu—not I."
sentences.idiomatic: Li Ling replied, "That was another man, Li Xu—not me."

sentences.chinese: 李緒本漢塞外都尉，居奚侯城，匈奴攻之，緒降，而單于客遇緒，常坐陵上。
sentences.literal: Li Xu had originally been Han frontier commandant beyond the barrier, dwelling at Xihou city; the Xiongnu attacked it, Xu surrendered, and the Chanyu treated Xu as a guest, always seating him above Ling.
sentences.idiomatic: Li Xu had been a Han frontier commandant at Xihou; when the Xiongnu took the place he surrendered and won such favor that the Chanyu seated him above Li Ling at feasts.

sentences.chinese: 陵痛其家以李緒而誅，使人刺殺緒。
sentences.literal: Ling grieved that his family because of Li Xu was executed; he sent men to stab and kill Xu.
sentences.idiomatic: Li Ling blamed Li Xu for the slaughter of his kin and had him assassinated.

sentences.chinese: 大閼氏欲殺陵，單于匿之北方，大閼氏死乃還。
sentences.literal: The great Lady wished to kill Ling; the Chanyu hid him in the north; when the great Lady died he then returned.
sentences.idiomatic: The Chanyu's chief wife wanted Li Ling dead; the Chanyu hid him in the far north until she died and he could return.

sentences.chinese: 單于壯陵，以女妻之，立為右校王，衛律為丁靈王，皆貴用事。
sentences.literal: The Chanyu thought Ling stalwart, gave him a daughter as wife, established him as king of the right commandant, Wei Lu as king of the Dingling—both were honored and wielded power.
sentences.idiomatic: The Chanyu admired him, married a daughter to him, and created him king of the right commandant; Wei Lu was made king of the Dingling—both rose to high office at court.

sentences.chinese: 衛律者，父本長水胡人。
sentences.literal: As for Wei Lu, his father was originally a Hu man of Changshui.
sentences.idiomatic: Wei Lu's father had been a Hu tribesman enrolled in the Changshui corps.

sentences.chinese: 律生長漢，善協律都尉李延年，延年薦言律使匈奴。
sentences.literal: Lu grew up in the Han and was on good terms with music-commandant Li Yannian; Yannian recommended Lu for a mission to the Xiongnu.
sentences.idiomatic: Wei Lu was raised at court, befriended the music-commandant Li Yannian, and was sent to the Xiongnu on Yannian's recommendation.

sentences.chinese: 使還，會延年家收，律懼並誅，亡還降匈奴。
sentences.literal: When his mission returned, it happened that Yannian's family was seized; Lu feared being executed together, fled back, and surrendered to the Xiongnu.
sentences.idiomatic: On his return he learned that Li Yannian's family had been arrested; fearing he would be executed as an accomplice, he fled to the steppe and defected.

sentences.chinese: 匈奴愛之，常在單于左右。
sentences.literal: The Xiongnu loved him; he was constantly at the Chanyu's left and right.
sentences.idiomatic: The Xiongnu valued him and kept him always at the Chanyu's side.

sentences.chinese: 陵居外，有大事，乃入議。
sentences.literal: Ling dwelt outside; when there was a great affair, then he entered to deliberate.
sentences.idiomatic: Li Ling lived apart from court and was summoned only for weighty councils of state.

sentences.chinese: 昭帝立，大將軍霍光、左將軍上官桀輔政，素與陵善，遣陵故人隴西任立政等三人俱至匈奴招陵。
sentences.literal: When Emperor Zhao was established, Supreme Commander Huo Guang and General of the Left Shangguan Jie assisted government; they had long been on good terms with Ling and dispatched Ling's old acquaintance Ren Lizheng of Longxi and two others together to the Xiongnu to summon Ling.
sentences.idiomatic: After Emperor Zhao came to the throne, Huo Guang and Shangguan Jie—who had long been friends of Li Ling—sent Ren Lizheng of Longxi and two other old comrades to the Xiongnu to persuade him to return.

sentences.chinese: 立政等至，單于置酒賜漢使者，李陵、衛律皆侍坐。
sentences.literal: When Lizheng and the others arrived, the Chanyu set out wine and feasted the Han envoys; Li Ling and Wei Lu both attended sitting.
sentences.idiomatic: The Chanyu gave a banquet for the Han envoys; Li Ling and Wei Lu both had seats at the table.

sentences.chinese: 立政等見陵，未得私語，即目視陵，而數數自循其刀環，握其足，陰諭之，言可還歸漢也。
sentences.literal: Lizheng and the others saw Ling but could not obtain private speech; then they gazed at Ling, and repeatedly themselves stroked their sword rings, grasped their feet, secretly instructing him, saying he could return to the Han.
sentences.idiomatic: Unable to speak alone with Li Ling, Ren Lizheng caught his eye, fingered the ring on his sword hilt, and tapped his own foot—signals that he could still go home to Han.

sentences.chinese: 後陵、律持牛酒勞漢使，博飲，兩人皆胡服椎結。
sentences.literal: Afterward Ling and Lu brought oxen and wine to console the Han envoy; they gambled and drank; both wore Hu dress with topknots.
sentences.idiomatic: Later Li Ling and Wei Lu brought beef and wine to entertain the envoys over dice and wine, both dressed as Xiongnu with hair in topknots.

sentences.chinese: 立政大言曰：「漢已大赦，中國安樂，主上富於春秋，霍子孟、上官少叔用事。」
sentences.literal: Lizheng spoke in a loud voice, saying, "The Han has greatly pardoned; the central states are peaceful and happy; the lord is rich in spring and autumn; Huo Zimeng and Shangguan Shaoshu wield power."
sentences.idiomatic: Ren Lizheng said loudly for all to hear: "The Han has proclaimed a general amnesty; the realm is at peace; the young emperor thrives; Huo Guang and Shangguan Jie hold the reins of government."

sentences.chinese: 以此言微動之。
sentences.literal: With these words he subtly moved him.
sentences.idiomatic: He meant the news to stir Li Ling without saying so outright.

sentences.chinese: 陵墨不應，孰視而自循其髪，答曰：「吾已胡服矣！」
sentences.literal: Ling remained silent and did not answer; he stared fixedly and himself stroked his hair, replying, "I have already put on Hu dress!"
sentences.idiomatic: Li Ling said nothing for a long moment, then ran a hand through his hair and answered softly, "I already wear the dress of the steppe."

sentences.chinese: 有頃，律起更衣，立政曰：「咄，少卿良苦！
sentences.literal: After a while Lu rose to change clothes; Lizheng said, "Tut, Shaoqing, how bitterly hard!
sentences.idiomatic: Presently Wei Lu left to change his robe. Ren Lizheng leaned in: "Shaoqing, you have suffered enough!

sentences.chinese: 霍子孟、上官少叔謝女。」
sentences.literal: Huo Zimeng and Shangguan Shaoshu greet you."
sentences.idiomatic: Huo Guang and Shangguan Jie send their regards."

sentences.chinese: 陵曰：「霍與上官無恙乎？」
sentences.literal: Ling said, "Are Huo and Shangguan without illness?"
sentences.idiomatic: Li Ling asked, "Are Huo Guang and Shangguan Jie well?"

sentences.chinese: 立政曰：「請少卿來歸故鄉，毋憂富貴。」
sentences.literal: Lizheng said, "We beg Shaoqing to come return to your old home—do not worry about wealth and honor."
sentences.idiomatic: Ren Lizheng answered, "Come home, Shaoqing—rank and riches need not concern you."

sentences.chinese: 陵字立政曰：「少公，歸易耳，恐再辱，奈何！」
sentences.literal: Ling by courtesy name addressed Lizheng, saying, "Shaogong, returning is easy, but I fear a second humiliation—what to do!"
sentences.idiomatic: Li Ling said, "Shaogong, going back would be easy; living through another disgrace would not."

sentences.chinese: 語未卒，衛律還，頗聞餘語，曰：「李少卿賢者，不獨居一國。
sentences.literal: Before the words were finished Lu returned, having overheard part of the remaining speech, and said, "Li Shaoqing is a worthy man; he does not dwell alone in one state.
sentences.idiomatic: Wei Lu walked in before Li Ling had finished and caught the tail end of the talk. "A worthy such as Li Shaoqing," he said, "need not belong to one realm alone.

sentences.chinese: 范蠡徧遊天下，由余去戎入秦，今何語之親也！」
sentences.literal: Fan Li roamed all under heaven; You Yu left the Rong and entered Qin—why now such intimate speech!"
sentences.idiomatic: Fan Li wandered the world; You Yu left the Rong for Qin—why this whispered intimacy?"

sentences.chinese: 因罷去。
sentences.literal: Thereupon he stopped and left.
sentences.idiomatic: With that he broke off the meeting and withdrew.

sentences.chinese: 立政隨謂陵曰：「亦有意乎？」
sentences.literal: Lizheng followed and said to Ling, "Do you also have the intent?"
sentences.idiomatic: Ren Lizheng caught up with Li Ling and murmured, "Will you come?"

sentences.chinese: 陵曰：「丈夫不能再辱。」
sentences.literal: Ling said, "A man cannot be humiliated twice."
sentences.idiomatic: Li Ling replied, "A gentleman does not endure a second shame."

sentences.chinese: 陵在匈奴二十餘年，元平元年病死。
sentences.literal: Ling was among the Xiongnu more than twenty years; in the first year of Yuanping he died of illness.
sentences.idiomatic: Li Ling lived more than twenty years among the Xiongnu and died of illness in 74 BCE.

sentences.chinese: 蘇建，杜陵人也。
sentences.literal: Su Jian was a man of Duling.
sentences.idiomatic: Su Jian came from Duling near the capital.

sentences.chinese: 以校尉從大將軍青擊匈奴，封平陵侯。
sentences.literal: As colonel he followed Supreme Commander Qing in striking the Xiongnu and was enfeoffed as Marquis of Pingling.
sentences.idiomatic: He served Wei Qing as a colonel against the Xiongnu and was ennobled as Marquis of Pingling.

sentences.chinese: 以將軍築朔方。
sentences.literal: As general he built Shuofang.
sentences.idiomatic: As a general officer he supervised construction of the Shuofang frontier commandery.

sentences.chinese: 後以衛尉為游擊將軍，從大將軍出朔方。
sentences.literal: Later as commandant of the guards he was mobile-strike general, following the supreme commander out of Shuofang.
sentences.idiomatic: He was later made mobile-strike general while commandant of the guards and again marched from Shuofang under Wei Qing.

sentences.chinese: 後一歲，以右將軍再從大將軍出定襄，亡翕侯，失軍當斬，贖為庶人。
sentences.literal: One year later as general of the right he again followed the supreme commander out of Dingxiang, lost Marquis Xi, lost the army—liable to execution—and ransomed to become a commoner.
sentences.idiomatic: The following year, as general of the right on another Dingxiang campaign, he lost the Xi marquis Zhao Xin and his entire command—capital crimes commuted to commoner status by ransom.

sentences.chinese: 其後為代郡太守，卒官。
sentences.literal: Afterward he was governor of Dai commandery and died in office.
sentences.idiomatic: He ended his career as governor of Dai and died in that post.

sentences.chinese: 有三子：嘉為奉車都尉，賢為騎都尉，中子武最知名。
sentences.literal: He had three sons: Jia was chariot commandant, Xian was cavalry commandant; the middle son Wu was most renowned.
sentences.idiomatic: He had three sons—Jia, who became chariot commandant; Xian, cavalry commandant; and the middle son Wu, who became the most famous of the three.

sentences.chinese: 武字子卿，少以父任，兄弟並為郎，稍遷至栘中廄監。
sentences.literal: Wu, courtesy name Ziqing, in youth by his father's privilege, brothers together were gentlemen-attendants, gradually promoted to Yizhong stable supervisor.
sentences.idiomatic: Su Wu, courtesy name Ziqing, entered official life through his father's rank; he and his brothers all became gentlemen-attendants, and he rose in time to supervisor of the imperial stables at Yizhong.

sentences.chinese: 時漢連伐胡，數通使相窺觀，匈奴留漢使郭吉、路充國等，前後十餘輩。
sentences.literal: At the time the Han repeatedly attacked the Hu and frequently sent envoys to spy on each other; the Xiongnu detained Han envoys Guo Ji, Lu Chongguo, and others—more than ten parties in succession.
sentences.idiomatic: The Han were campaigning constantly against the Hu and exchanged hostage-like embassies for reconnaissance; the Xiongnu had held more than ten Han missions, including Guo Ji and Lu Chongguo.

sentences.chinese: 匈奴使來，漢亦留之以相當。
sentences.literal: When Xiongnu envoys came, the Han also detained them to match.
sentences.idiomatic: Whenever Xiongnu envoys arrived, the Han detained them in turn as counter-hostages.

sentences.chinese: 天漢元年，且鞮侯單于初立，恐漢襲之，乃曰：「漢天子我丈人行也。」
sentences.literal: In the first year of Tianhan the Chanyu Juandi-hou was newly established; fearing the Han would strike him, he then said, "The Han Son of Heaven is of my father's generation in courtesy."
sentences.idiomatic: In 100 BCE the new Chanyu Juandi-hou, fearing a Han strike, declared that he honored the Han emperor as an elder.

sentences.chinese: 盡歸漢使路充國等。
sentences.literal: He entirely returned Han envoys Lu Chongguo and others.
sentences.idiomatic: He released all detained Han envoys, including Lu Chongguo.

sentences.chinese: 武帝嘉其義，乃遣武以中郎將使持節送匈奴使留在漢者，因厚賂單于，答其善意。
sentences.literal: Emperor Wu praised his righteousness and thereupon dispatched Wu as gentleman of the palace bearing credentials to escort the Xiongnu envoys detained in the Han, and thereby richly bribed the Chanyu, replying to his good intent.
sentences.idiomatic: Emperor Wu welcomed the gesture and sent Su Wu as gentleman of the palace with the imperial staff to escort the Xiongnu envoys home, bearing lavish gifts for the Chanyu in return.

sentences.chinese: 武與副中郎將張勝及假吏常惠等募士斥候百餘人俱。
sentences.literal: Wu together with deputy gentleman of the palace Zhang Sheng and acting clerk Chang Hui and others, with recruited soldiers and scouts numbering more than a hundred, went together.
sentences.idiomatic: Su Wu set out with his deputy Zhang Sheng, the clerk Chang Hui, and a train of more than a hundred hired guards and scouts.

sentences.chinese: 既至匈奴，置幣遺單于。
sentences.literal: Having reached the Xiongnu, they set out gifts and presented them to the Chanyu.
sentences.idiomatic: On arrival they presented the diplomatic gifts to the Chanyu.

sentences.chinese: 單于益驕，非漢所望也。
sentences.literal: The Chanyu grew all the more arrogant—not what the Han had hoped.
sentences.idiomatic: The Chanyu grew only more arrogant—far from the courtesy the Han had expected.

sentences.chinese: 方欲發使送武等，會緱王與長水虞常等謀反匈奴中。
sentences.literal: Just as they were about to dispatch envoys to escort Wu and the others, it happened that the King of Gou and Changshui Yu Chang and others plotted rebellion within the Xiongnu.
sentences.idiomatic: The Chanyu was about to send Su Wu home when Gou Wang and Yu Chang of the Changshui Hu plotted a coup on the steppe.

sentences.chinese: 緱王者，昆邪王姊子也，與昆邪王俱降漢，後隨浞野侯沒胡中。
sentences.literal: The King of Gou was the elder sister's son of King Hunye; together with King Hunye he had surrendered to the Han, afterward following the Zhuoye Marquis lost among the Hu.
sentences.idiomatic: Gou Wang was a nephew of the surrendering King Hunye; he had later been stranded among the Xiongnu with Zhao Ponu's lost column.

sentences.chinese: 及衛律所將降者，陰相與謀劫單于母閼氏歸漢。
sentences.literal: Together with those surrendered whom Wei Lu had led, they secretly together plotted to seize the Chanyu's mother the Lady and return to the Han.
sentences.idiomatic: They conspired in secret with other Han defectors under Wei Lu to kidnap the Chanyu's mother and ride back to the empire.

sentences.chinese: 會武等至匈奴，虞常在漢時素與副張勝相知，私候勝曰：「聞漢天子甚怨衛律，常能為漢伏弩射殺之。
sentences.literal: When Wu and the others reached the Xiongnu, Yu Chang while in the Han had long been acquainted with deputy Zhang Sheng; he privately visited Sheng, saying, "I have heard the Han Son of Heaven greatly resents Wei Lu; Chang can for the Han lie in ambush with crossbow and shoot him dead.
sentences.idiomatic: Yu Chang had known Zhang Sheng in Chang'an; he came to him privately and said, "The emperor hates Wei Lu. Let me ambush him with a crossbow for Han.

sentences.chinese: 吾母與弟在漢，幸蒙其賞賜。」
sentences.literal: My mother and younger brother are in the Han; I hope to receive their rewards and gifts."
sentences.idiomatic: My mother and brother still live in Han—perhaps the court will reward us."

sentences.chinese: 張勝許之，以貨物與常。
sentences.literal: Zhang Sheng promised it and gave goods to Yu.
sentences.idiomatic: Zhang Sheng agreed and gave him supplies.

sentences.chinese: 後月餘，單于出獵，獨閼氏子弟在。
sentences.literal: More than a month later the Chanyu went out hunting; only the Lady's sons and younger kin were present.
sentences.idiomatic: A month later the Chanyu rode out on a hunt, leaving only the queen's household at court.

sentences.chinese: 虞常等七十餘人欲發，其一人夜亡，告之。
sentences.literal: Yu Chang and more than seventy men wished to rise; one of them fled by night and reported it.
sentences.idiomatic: More than seventy conspirators were ready to move when one man slipped away in the night and betrayed them.

sentences.chinese: 單于子弟發兵與戰。
sentences.literal: The Chanyu's sons and younger kin raised troops and fought them.
sentences.idiomatic: The Chanyu's kinsmen called out the guard and fought them in the camp.

sentences.chinese: 緱王等皆死，虞常生得。
sentences.literal: The King of Gou and the rest all died; Yu Chang was captured alive.
sentences.idiomatic: Gou Wang and his party were killed; Yu Chang was taken alive.

sentences.chinese: 單于使衛律治其事。
sentences.literal: The Chanyu ordered Wei Lu to handle the affair.
sentences.idiomatic: The Chanyu put Wei Lu in charge of the investigation.

sentences.chinese: 張勝聞之，恐前語發，以狀語武。
sentences.literal: Zhang Sheng hearing it, feared the earlier words would come out, and told the circumstances to Wu.
sentences.idiomatic: Zhang Sheng, afraid his part would be exposed, confessed the whole plot to Su Wu.

sentences.chinese: 武曰：「事如此，此必及我。
sentences.literal: Wu said, "The affair being thus, this is sure to reach me.
sentences.idiomatic: Su Wu said, "Matters have come to this—they will drag me in as well.

sentences.chinese: 見犯乃死，重負國。」
sentences.literal: If I wait until I am violated and then die, I will have doubly failed the state."
sentences.idiomatic: To endure outrage and only then die would shame the empire twice over."

sentences.chinese: 欲自殺，勝、惠共止之。
sentences.literal: He wished to kill himself; Sheng and Hui together stopped him.
sentences.idiomatic: He tried to kill himself, but Zhang Sheng and Chang Hui restrained him.

sentences.chinese: 虞常果引張勝。
sentences.literal: Yu Chang indeed implicated Zhang Sheng.
sentences.idiomatic: Under torture Yu Chang denounced Zhang Sheng.

sentences.chinese: 單于怒，召諸貴人議，欲殺漢使者。
sentences.literal: The Chanyu was angry, summoned the various nobles to deliberate, and wished to kill the Han envoys.
sentences.idiomatic: The Chanyu in his rage called a council of nobles and demanded the Han envoys' deaths.

sentences.chinese: 左伊秩訾曰：「即謀單于，何以復加？
sentences.literal: The Left Yizhi said, "If the crime is plotting against the Chanyu, what greater penalty can be added?
sentences.idiomatic: The Left Yizhi objected: "They aimed at the Chanyu himself—execution is already the limit of the law.

sentences.chinese: 宜皆降之。」
sentences.literal: It is fitting all be made to surrender."
sentences.idiomatic: Better force them all to submit."

sentences.chinese: 單于使衛律召武受辭，武謂惠等：「屈節辱命，雖生，何面目以歸漢！」
sentences.literal: The Chanyu ordered Wei Lu to summon Wu to receive interrogation; Wu said to Hui and the others, "Bending the integrity and shaming the commission—though alive, what face to return to the Han!"
sentences.idiomatic: Wei Lu was sent to take Su Wu's deposition. Su Wu told Chang Hui and the rest, "I will not disgrace my charge and then crawl home alive."

sentences.chinese: 引佩刀自刺。
sentences.literal: He drew his belt knife and stabbed himself.
sentences.idiomatic: He drew his court dagger and drove it into his breast.

sentences.chinese: 衛律驚，自抱持武，馳召毉。
sentences.literal: Wei Lu was alarmed, himself embraced and held Wu, and galloped to summon a physician.
sentences.idiomatic: Wei Lu caught him as he fell and sent gallopers for a physician.

sentences.chinese: 鑿地為坎，置煴火，覆武其上，蹈其背以出血。
sentences.literal: They dug earth into a pit, set a smoldering fire, covered Wu above it, and trod his back to let blood out.
sentences.idiomatic: They dug a shallow pit, laid a bed of warm ashes, stretched Su Wu on top, and stamped his back to free the wound of blood.

sentences.chinese: 武氣絕半日，復息。
sentences.literal: Wu's breath ceased half a day; then he breathed again.
sentences.idiomatic: He lay without breath for half a day before life returned.

sentences.chinese: 惠等哭，輿歸營。
sentences.literal: Hui and the others wept and carried him in a litter back to camp.
sentences.idiomatic: Chang Hui and the others wept and carried him on a litter to his tent.

sentences.chinese: 單于壯其節，朝夕遣人候問武，而收系張勝。
sentences.literal: The Chanyu thought his integrity stalwart; morning and evening he sent men to inquire after Wu, while arresting and binding Zhang Sheng.
sentences.idiomatic: The Chanyu admired his courage and sent messengers morning and night to ask after him, while Zhang Sheng was thrown in chains.

sentences.chinese: 武益愈，單于使使曉武。
sentences.literal: Wu gradually recovered; the Chanyu sent envoys to enlighten Wu.
sentences.idiomatic: When Su Wu began to mend, the Chanyu sent men to reason with him.

sentences.chinese: 會論虞常，欲因此時降武。
sentences.literal: They convened to judge Yu Chang, wishing by this occasion to make Wu surrender.
sentences.idiomatic: They staged a public trial of Yu Chang, intending to break Su Wu's will at the same moment.

sentences.chinese: 劍斬虞常已，律曰：「漢使張勝謀殺單于近臣，當死，單于募降者赦罪。」
sentences.literal: Having with the sword beheaded Yu Chang, Lu said, "Han envoy Zhang Sheng plotted to kill the Chanyu's close minister—liable to death; the Chanyu seeks those who surrender to pardon crime."
sentences.idiomatic: Wei Lu beheaded Yu Chang with a stroke, then announced, "Zhang Sheng plotted murder of a great officer of the Chanyu—capital crime. The Chanyu offers pardon to those who yield."

sentences.chinese: 舉劍欲擊之，勝請降。
sentences.literal: He raised the sword intending to strike him; Sheng requested to surrender.
sentences.idiomatic: He raised the blade over Zhang Sheng; Zhang cried out that he submitted.

sentences.chinese: 律謂武曰：「副有罪，當相坐。」
sentences.literal: Lu said to Wu, "The deputy has guilt; you ought to share the punishment."
sentences.idiomatic: Wei Lu turned to Su Wu: "Your deputy is guilty—you share his fate."
